lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਤ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਸਾਕਤ ਬਾਜੀ ਹਾਰੀ ਜੀਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: satigur milai ta guramat paaeeai saakat baajee haaree jeeo |3|
lines.english: Meeting the True Guru, the Guru's Teachings are obtained; the faithless cynic loses the game of life. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਕਉ ਇਹੁ ਜਗੁ ਬਪੁੜੋ ਇਨਿ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਗਤਿ ਵਿਸਾਰੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: janam maran kau ihu jag bapurro in doojai bhagat visaaree jeeo |
lines.english: This wretched world is caught in birth and death; in the love of duality, it has forgotten devotional worship of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬੰਧਨ ਤੋੜਿ ਨਿਰਾਰੇ ਬਹੁੜਿ ਨ ਗਰਭ ਮਝਾਰੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: satigur bandhan torr niraare bahurr na garabh majhaaree jeeo |
lines.english: Breaking my bonds, the True Guru has set me free, and I shall not be cast into the womb of reincarnation again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗਿਆਨ ਰਤਨੁ ਪਰਗਾਸਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਮਨਿ ਵਸਿਆ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ ਜੀਉ ॥੪॥੮॥
lines.translit: naanak giaan ratan paragaasiaa har man vasiaa nirankaaree jeeo |4|8|
lines.english: O Nanak, the jewel of spiritual wisdom shines forth, and the Lord, the Formless Lord, dwells within my mind. ||4||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਛੋਡਹੁ ਵੇਸੁ ਭੇਖ ਚਤੁਰਾਈ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਇਹੁ ਫਲੁ ਨਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: chhoddahu ves bhekh chaturaaee dubidhaa ihu fal naahee jeeo |1|
lines.english: Renounce costumes, disguises and clever tricks; this fruit is not obtained by duplicity. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਰਠਿ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: soratth mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Sorat'h, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਜਲ ਨਿਧਿ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਤੁਮ ਜਗਿ ਆਏ ਸੋ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਗੁਰ ਪਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: jis jal nidh kaaran tum jag aae so amrit gur paahee jeeo |
lines.english: The treasure of the Name, for which you have come into the world - that Ambrosial Nectar is with the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਰੇ ਥਿਰੁ ਰਹੁ ਮਤੁ ਕਤ ਜਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: man re thir rahu mat kat jaahee jeeo |
lines.english: O my mind, remain steady, and do not wander away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਢੂਢਤ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਾਵਹਿ ਘਰਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਘਟ ਮਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: baahar dtoodtat bahut dukh paaveh ghar amrit ghatt maahee jeeo | rahaau |
lines.english: By searching around on the outside, you shall only suffer great pain; the Ambrosial Nectar is found within the home of your own being. ||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਗੁਣ ਛੋਡਿ ਗੁਣਾ ਕਉ ਧਾਵਹੁ ਕਰਿ ਅਵਗੁਣ ਪਛੁਤਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: avagun chhodd gunaa kau dhaavahu kar avagun pachhutaahee jeeo |
lines.english: Renounce corruption, and seek virtue; committing sins, you shall only come to regret and repent.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਮੈਲੁ ਲੋਭ ਬਹੁ ਝੂਠੇ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਨਾਵਹੁ ਕਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: antar mail lobh bahu jhootthe baahar naavahu kaahee jeeo |
lines.english: Within you is the great filth of greed and falsehood; why do you bother to wash your body on the outside?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰ ਅਪਸਰ ਕੀ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਣਹਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਕੀਚ ਬੁਡਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sar apasar kee saar na jaaneh fir fir keech buddaahee jeeo |2|
lines.english: You do not know the difference between good and evil; again and again, you sink into the mud. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਰਮਲ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਹੁ ਸਦ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਤਰ ਕੀ ਗਤਿ ਤਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: niramal naam japahu sad guramukh antar kee gat taahee jeeo |3|
lines.english: Chant the Immaculate Naam, the Name of the Lord always, under Guru's Instruction; only then will your innermost being be emancipated. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਉ ਰਾਖਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਜੀਉ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਲਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥੪॥੯॥
lines.translit: jiau bhaavai tiau raakhahu har jeeo jan naanak sabad salaahee jeeo |4|9|
lines.english: As it pleases You, You preserve me, Dear Lord; servant Nanak sings the Praises of Your Shabad. ||4||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਹਰਿ ਲੋਭੁ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਕੂੜੁ ਤਿਆਗਹੁ ਸਚੁ ਗੁਰ ਬਚਨੀ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਹੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: parahar lobh nindaa koorr tiaagahu sach gur bachanee fal paahee jeeo |
lines.english: Let greed and slander be far away from you, and renounce falsehood; through the True Word of the Guru's Shabad, you shall obtain the true fruit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਰਠਿ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਪੰਚਪਦੇ ॥
lines.translit: soratth mahalaa 1 panchapade |
lines.english: Sorat'h, First Mehl, Panch-Padhay:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨਾ ਘਰੁ ਮੂਸਤ ਰਾਖਿ ਨ ਸਾਕਹਿ ਕੀ ਪਰ ਘਰੁ ਜੋਹਨ ਲਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: apanaa ghar moosat raakh na saakeh kee par ghar johan laagaa |
lines.english: You cannot save your own home from being plundered; why do you spy on the houses of others?

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰੁ ਦਰੁ ਰਾਖਹਿ ਜੇ ਰਸੁ ਚਾਖਹਿ ਜੋ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਲਾਗਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: ghar dar raakheh je ras chaakheh jo guramukh sevak laagaa |1|
lines.english: That Gurmukh who joins himself to the Guru's service, saves his own home, and tastes the Lord's Nectar. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿ ਅਨ ਰਸ ਲੋਭਾਨੇ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਹਿ ਅਭਾਗਾ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: naam visaar an ras lobhaane fir pachhutaeh abhaagaa | rahaau |
lines.english: Forgetting the Naam, the Name of the Lord, one is involved with other tastes; the unfortunate wretch shall come to regret it in the end. ||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਰੇ ਸਮਝੁ ਕਵਨ ਮਤਿ ਲਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: man re samajh kavan mat laagaa |
lines.english: O mind, you must realize what your intellect is focused on.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਤ ਕਉ ਹਰਖ ਜਾਤ ਕਉ ਰੋਵਹਿ ਇਹੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਨਾਲੇ ਲਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: aavat kau harakh jaat kau roveh ihu dukh sukh naale laagaa |
lines.english: When things come, he is pleased, but when they go, he weeps and wails; this pain and pleasure remains attached to him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਦੁਖ ਸੁਖ ਭੋਗਿ ਭੋਗਾਵੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੋ ਅਨਰਾਗਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aape dukh sukh bhog bhogaavai guramukh so anaraagaa |2|
lines.english: The Lord Himself causes him to enjoy pleasure and endure pain; the Gurmukh, however, remains unaffected. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹਿਤ ਜਿਨਿ ਇਹੁ ਰਸੁ ਖੋਇਆ ਜਾ ਸਾਕਤ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਲਾਗਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: maaeaa mohit jin ihu ras khoeaa jaa saakat duramat laagaa |3|
lines.english: One who is lured by Maya loses this juice; that faithless cynic is tied to his evil-mindedness. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸ ਊਪਰਿ ਅਵਰੁ ਕਿਆ ਕਹੀਐ ਜਿਨਿ ਪੀਆ ਸੋ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: har ras aoopar avar kiaa kaheeai jin peea so tripataagaa |
lines.english: What else can be said to be above the subtle essence of the Lord? One who drinks it in is satisfied and satiated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਕਾ ਜੀਉ ਪਵਨਪਤਿ ਦੇਹੀ ਦੇਹੀ ਮਹਿ ਦੇਉ ਸਮਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: man kaa jeeo pavanapat dehee dehee meh deo samaagaa |
lines.english: The Lord is the life of the mind, the Master of the breath of life; the Divine Lord is contained in the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਤੂ ਦੇਹਿ ਤ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਗਾਈ ਮਨੁ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤੈ ਹਰਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: je too dehi ta har ras gaaee man tripatai har liv laagaa |4|
lines.english: If You so bless us, Lord, then we sing Your Praises; the mind is satisfied and fulfilled, lovingly attached to the Lord. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਜਮ ਭਉ ਭਾਗਾ ॥
lines.translit: saadhasangat meh har ras paaeeai gur miliai jam bhau bhaagaa |
lines.english: In the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, the subtle essence of the Lord is obtained; meeting the Guru, the fear of death departs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਪਾਏ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਭਾਗਾ ॥੫॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: naanak raam naam jap guramukh har paae masatak bhaagaa |5|10|
lines.english: O Nanak, chant the Name of the Lord, as Gurmukh; you shall obtain the Lord, and realize your pre-ordained destiny. ||5||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਰਠਿ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: soratth mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Sorat'h, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਬ ਜੀਆ ਸਿਰਿ ਲੇਖੁ ਧੁਰਾਹੂ ਬਿਨੁ ਲੇਖੈ ਨਹੀ ਕੋਈ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: sarab jeea sir lekh dhuraahoo bin lekhai nahee koee jeeo |
lines.english: Destiny, pre-ordained by the Lord, looms over the heads of all beings; no one is without this pre-ordained destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਅਲੇਖੁ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਦੇਖੈ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਚਲਾਏ ਸੋਈ ਜੀਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aap alekh kudarat kar dekhai hukam chalaae soee jeeo |1|
lines.english: Only He Himself is beyond destiny; creating the creation by His creative power, He beholds it, and causes His Command to be followed. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਰੇ ਰਾਮ ਜਪਹੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: man re raam japahu sukh hoee |
lines.english: O mind, chant the Name of the Lord, and be at peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਚਰਨ ਸਰੇਵਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਦਾਤਾ ਭੁਗਤਾ ਸੋਈ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ahinis gur ke charan sarevahu har daataa bhugataa soee | rahaau |
lines.english: Day and night, serve at the Guru's feet; the Lord is the Giver, and the Enjoyer. ||Pause||
